description: Returning a typed nil pointer through an interface return value, so an `err != nil` check is unexpectedly true.
body: Failure mode: a function returns a concrete *MyError that is nil but typed as the error interface; the interface holds a (type, nil-value) pair so `if err != nil` is true even though no error occurred. Detection signals: callers report an error with a nil-ish message; `errors.Is(err, nil)` confusion; the bug appears only when a custom error type is returned. Do not return a typed nil pointer as an interface. Safer fallback: declare the return as error and return literal nil (not a nil *T) on success, or check the concrete value before returning; add a test asserting the success path yields err == nil.
